observed_failure.surface: GitHub Actions focused and full TestMesh
observed_failure.prior_local_claim: generated SkillGuard V2 contract was current
observed_failure.observed_result: the first CI attempt produced different contract hashes across checkouts; after source hashes were normalized, a second CI attempt produced the same semantic hashes but Git still rewrote committed generated JSON to CRLF on Windows, so byte-exact parity remained stale

generalized_repair.rule: Normalize UTF-8 text CRLF/CR/LF before source hashing; preserve exact bytes for binary files; force deterministic generated JSON artifacts to LF at the Git checkout boundary.
generalized_repair.openspec_scenario: Same text checkout uses platform line endings and clean Windows checkout preserves canonical generated bytes
generalized_repair.negative_boundary: A true binary-byte change remains stale and cannot be normalized away.

additional_environment_boundary.finding: The pinned FlowGuard runtime imports standard-library tomllib and therefore cannot execute on Python 3.10.
additional_environment_boundary.disposition: Declare Python 3.11+ and test 3.11/3.12 instead of injecting an undocumented compatibility shim.

revalidation_evidence.clean_windows_checkout: f94c9e5 with core.autocrlf=true; read-only compile wrote zero files
revalidation_evidence.focused_test_mesh: 109 passed before the Git checkout boundary regression and 110 passed in final full V2 partition
revalidation_evidence.full_test_mesh: 110 V2 plus 73 legacy passed with zero skips; parent report 755A6952654BCEB98348365BE17E47D3198D637DB3A1AA4DF2078A93DECDF9B8
revalidation_evidence.external_target: Autonomous UI run-478f9363ccad2bb2977b under Guard source hash 4AF0446CC399F4D80C7EE5EAE82D9DEB284E524712D08D44AF1E910AA42DDAB5
revalidation_evidence.github_actions: run 29088309097 passed Windows/Linux, Python 3.11/3.12, clean-install, and full legacy jobs

claim_boundary: Current evidence proves the repaired source and generated-artifact representation class locally and in the named Windows/Linux CI matrix. It does not prove GitHub release publication, future platform behavior, or unrelated portfolio targets.
